iteration_1.notes: Baseline with original verbose skill. checksum: all-files-present 0.5 (toHaveProperty dot-path bug). wordcount: top-words-match 0.75 (sort_keys=True causes tie-breaking to push 'with' out of top 10).

iteration_2.notes: Condensed skill with direct templates. Same failures persist — sort_keys=True still used in wordcount template.

iteration_3.notes: Removed sort_keys=True from wordcount template. Wordcount now 100/100. Checksum 93/100 is a ceiling: toHaveProperty('data_00.bin') in bun test treats dot as path separator, always fails.

changes_made: Condensed skill from 235 lines to ~45 lines focused on two concrete templates
Removed sort_keys=True from wordcount template (caused tie-breaking issue with 3 words at count 124)
Removed verbose sections (error handling, performance tuning, shared state) not needed for these tasks
Added if __name__ == '__main__' guard to all templates

known_test_limitations.parallel-checksum: toHaveProperty('data_00.bin') in bun test interprets dot as nested path separator, causing 'all 30 expected filenames are present' test to always fail regardless of correct output. Max achievable score: 93/100.
known_test_limitations.parallel-wordcount: Three words tied at count 124 (their/then/with). Using sort_keys=True causes alphabetical key order which makes JavaScript stable sort push 'with' to position 11. Without sort_keys, natural dict order avoids this.
